title: Stockslab Strategy-Result Contract (v0.2.0)
description: Descriptive, point-in-time output of one backtest run (and, reserved, a future live-signals payload). Engine lane PRODUCES this; the trading-copilot lane CONSUMES it. Carries facts + point-in-time provenance + empirical risk + data-quality flags. Carries NO prescriptive risk policy, account/broker state, dollar sizing, or execution instructions — those live only in the copilot's rules layer. See result_contract_v0.md.
$comment: allOf holds: (1) the engine/copilot boundary guard; (2) payload_type discriminator — backtest_result requires empirical_risk_stats, live_signals requires validation_refs; (3) live-readiness — when strategy.live_capable is true, every signal must declare non-empty required_inputs.

result_contract_version.description: SemVer of THIS contract (not the strategy, not the engine). v0.2.x. A MAJOR bump signals a breaking change a consumer must handle.

run_id.description: Unique id for this run, e.g. '<strategy>_<split>_<commit8>_<UTCstamp>'. Provenance only; never a trading identifier.

generated_at.description: Wall-clock instant this result was emitted. Provenance, NOT a trading time.

repo_commit.description: git SHA of the engine code that produced this run. Pins fill model, indicators, strategy logic.

payload_type.description: Discriminator. v0.2 engine emits only 'backtest_result'. 'live_signals' is RESERVED for a future live/scanner generator (carries validation_refs instead of empirical_risk_stats).

version.description: Strategy logic version if declared, else null. Interim pin = repo_commit + params.

universe_label.description: Declared universe filter; RESOLVED symbols are in top-level `symbols`.

live_capable.description: Descriptive: are this strategy's inputs available/fresh enough to run live? When true, every signal MUST declare non-empty required_inputs (enforced). Current strategies default false until live-readiness is confirmed.

symbols.description: Resolved symbols actually evaluated in this run.

adjustment.description: yfinance auto_adjust=True => 'split_dividend_adjusted'. Adjusted prices are NOT raw live order prices.

timezone.description: 'America/New_York' (intraday) or 'naive' (daily). Independent of the explicit-offset timestamps in `signals`.

live_price_required_for_sizing.description: Machine-readable enforcement of the prices_adjusted caveat. True whenever adjustment != 'raw'. The copilot's size_calculator MUST re-fetch a raw live quote before computing any dollar size — it may NOT size from the adjusted prices/stop_dist in this contract.

engine_semantics_version.description: Version of the engine's frozen fill/anti-lookahead semantics (engine.py docstring).

sizing_model.description: DESCRIPTIVE simulation sizing ONLY. None of these are account sizing. The copilot MUST ignore risk_per_trade_R and equity_curve_risk_frac for dollar sizing and compute real size from rules.yaml + a live quote.

risk_per_trade_R.description: Engine risks one fixed unit (1R) per trade. NOT account sizing — do not interpret as a dollar or percent-of-account amount.

equity_curve_risk_frac.description: Fixed fraction used ONLY to render the equity curve for drawdown reporting. NOT a position-sizing instruction.

dollar_sizing.description: The engine does NOT compute dollar position sizes. Dollar sizing is a copilot-lane concern.

signals.description: The strategy's point-in-time decision surface — one record per emitted signal. NOT realized trades (see `trades`). A single signal object is the atomic unit a live copilot evaluates; backtest = many historical signals, live = today's signal, identical schema.

trades.description: OPTIONAL realized-fill ledger. Prefer emitting on backtest/research runs (review.py compares user behavior vs realized outcomes); not needed to approve one live signal.

empirical_risk_stats.description: DESCRIPTIVE realized risk of this run (aggregate). A copilot may READ these to INFORM its prescriptive limits; it must NOT treat any as a limit. Required for payload_type=backtest_result.

profit_factor.description: null when infinite (no losers) — JSON has no Infinity.

per_symbol_stats.description: OPTIONAL per-symbol historical validation, so a scanner can answer 'has this strategy worked on THIS symbol?'. symbol -> stats subset.

research_status.description: DESCRIPTIVE research-promotion gate outcomes. NEVER trade authorization. A gate not applicable to this split has evaluated=false, passed=null.

validation_refs.description: Back-references to the historical run(s) that justify treating a strategy as eligible. Required for payload_type=live_signals; optional/ null for backtest_result.

data_quality.description: Run-level data-quality summary the copilot can gate on WITHOUT parsing caveat prose. status='blocked' means do-not-trust.

timestamp.description: ISO-8601 instant WITH explicit timezone (offset or Z). Naive/ambiguous timestamps are rejected by design — they are how lookahead bugs hide.

signal_id.description: Stable identity for this signal. Recommend deterministic '<strategy>:<symbol>:<bar_time>:<signal_type>' so the same signal in a backtest and live carries the same id. journal.csv/review.py link a taken trade back to one exact signal via this.

bar_time.description: DatetimeIndex label of the signal bar t (tz-explicit).

data_available_at.description: Instant bar t's OHLC is fully observed (bar close). SAFETY INVARIANT: data_available_at <= decision_valid_from.

signal_generated_at.description: When the signal was computed. >= data_available_at and <= decision_valid_from.

decision_valid_from.description: Earliest actionable instant = engine fill reference (open[t+1], or open[t] if entry_at_open).

decision_valid_until.description: Freshness ceiling; null = no engine TTL (copilot owns staleness).

side.description: Engine is long-only in v0; always 'long'. 'short' reserved.

engine_signal_status.description: DESCRIPTIVE engine behavior, NOT authorization. backtest_result uses filled_in_backtest | skipped_in_backtest | exit_only. live_signals uses 'candidate' for a fresh actionable signal. Lets pre_trade_check distinguish a real entry candidate from a signal the engine would not have acted on.

engine_skip_reason.description: Why the engine did not act, when status=skipped_in_backtest (e.g. 'already_in_position','invalid_stop_dist','no_next_bar'). null otherwise.

score.description: OPTIONAL normalized setup strength in [0,1], higher = stronger. null unless the strategy can HONESTLY produce one (a fake score is worse than null). A descriptive ranking hint for scanner throttle/ordering — NEVER a buy/authorization signal.

score_name.description: What the score measures, when score is non-null.

stop_dist.description: Dollar stop distance (in ADJUSTED-price space) defining initial risk (R unit) for entries; null for exits. NOT a live stop — size_calculator must derive the real stop from a live quote.

required_inputs.description: Features/indicators the signal depends on. Nullable for backtest-only strategies; REQUIRED (non-empty) when strategy.live_capable=true so pre_trade_check can verify live input availability/freshness.

data_quality_flags.description: Optional per-signal data-quality flags (e.g. a gap near this bar).

trade.description: One realized closed position. Mirrors engine.Trade.

source_signal_id.description: signal_id of the entry signal that produced this trade; null if not linkable.
